# Rex

> Rex is a strongly-typed, pure functional programming language designed for embedding in Rust applications and as a reliable target for LLM-generated programs.

Rex programs are expression-oriented and side-effect free in the language itself. Hosts inject native Rust functions for effects and domain integration. Type inference, ADTs, type classes, and a prelude make Rex suitable for data transformation and workflow orchestration.

When generating Rex code, prefer consulting the LLM guidance and tutorial first, then confirm details in the language reference and specification.

## Start Here

- [Documentation Markdown Source (docs/src)](https://github.com/peterkelly/rex/tree/main/docs/src): Raw documentation markdown files used to build the docs site.
- [Documentation Home](https://peterkelly.github.io/rex/): Entry point for all Rex documentation.
- [LLM Guidance](https://peterkelly.github.io/rex/LLMS.html): Practical rules for generating valid Rex code quickly.
- [Tutorial Overview](https://peterkelly.github.io/rex/tutorial/): Guided learning path through language features.
- [Language Reference](https://peterkelly.github.io/rex/LANGUAGE.html): Compact syntax and feature reference.

## Core Reference

- [Specification](https://peterkelly.github.io/rex/SPEC.html): Locked semantics and edge cases.
- [Architecture](https://peterkelly.github.io/rex/ARCHITECTURE.html): Pipeline and crate responsibilities.
- [Embedding Rex](https://peterkelly.github.io/rex/EMBEDDING.html): Host integration patterns and safety checklist.
- [Memory Management](https://peterkelly.github.io/rex/MEMORY_MANAGEMENT.html): Runtime memory model details.

## Tutorial

- [Section 1: Basics](https://peterkelly.github.io/rex/tutorial/section1/): Syntax, expressions, functions, ADTs, records, and prelude usage.
- [Section 2: Advanced Topics](https://peterkelly.github.io/rex/tutorial/section2/): Inference, polymorphism, type classes, functor/applicative/monad.
- [Section 3: Worked Examples](https://peterkelly.github.io/rex/tutorial/section3/): End-to-end examples and small projects.

## Project and Tooling

- [GitHub Repository](https://github.com/peterkelly/rex): Source code for compiler pipeline, runtime, and tooling.
- [README](https://github.com/peterkelly/rex/blob/main/README.md): Quick project overview and CLI usage.
- [Contributing](https://peterkelly.github.io/rex/CONTRIBUTING.html): Development workflow and contribution guide.

## Optional

- [Demo Index](https://peterkelly.github.io/rex/demos/): Interactive algorithm demos.
- [VS Code Extension](https://github.com/peterkelly/rex/tree/main/rex-vscode): Editor integration for Rex via LSP.
